+- Impact: `Coach` and `Administrator` inherit `Member`, so the default overview refresh path calls `/api/self/profile`; that route ultimately requires a `member_profiles` row and throws `member_not_found` for non-member accounts. The frontend swallows the error, producing hidden failing requests/log noise on the landing view for two core roles.
+- Minimum actionable fix: Gate `loadSelf()` to true member profiles only, or make `/api/self/profile` role-aware and able to return a non-member summary for coach/admin sessions. Add a test using the real member service with expanded roles.
+
+### Medium
+
+#### 2. Reviewer-facing documentation is internally inconsistent and references missing/out-of-scope artifacts
+- Severity: Medium
+- Conclusion: Fail
+- Evidence: `C:\Hale\Eaglepoint\Task-75\repo\README.md:5`, `C:\Hale\Eaglepoint\Task-75\repo\README.md:31`, `C:\Hale\Eaglepoint\Task-75\repo\README.md:34`, `C:\Hale\Eaglepoint\Task-75\docs\api-spec.md:48`, `C:\Hale\Eaglepoint\Task-75\docs\api-spec.md:85`, `C:\Hale\Eaglepoint\Task-75\docs\api-spec.md:165`, `C:\Hale\Eaglepoint\Task-75\docs\design.md:130`
+- Impact: Static verification is harder than necessary because the README points to an absent `temp` benchmark on an external `E:` path, while the API/design docs describe a nonexistent `/api/auth/restore` route and incorrect auth semantics for `/api/auth/session`.
+- Minimum actionable fix: Remove external-path references, document only in-repo artifacts, replace `/api/auth/restore` with `/api/auth/session`, and align the unsigned/signed route descriptions with the implemented router.
+
+#### 3. Rate limiting is keyed only by source IP, which conflicts with the prompt’s multi-terminal offline operating model
+- Severity: Medium
+- Conclusion: Partial Fail
+- Evidence: `C:\Hale\Eaglepoint\Task-75\repo\backend\src\middleware.ts:78`, `C:\Hale\Eaglepoint\Task-75\repo\backend\src\middleware.ts:88`, `C:\Hale\Eaglepoint\Task-75\repo\backend\src\middleware.ts:127`, `C:\Hale\Eaglepoint\Task-75\docs\questions.md:97`, `C:\Hale\Eaglepoint\Task-75\docs\questions.md:103`
+- Impact: Shared kiosk/front-desk terminals behind one LAN IP can rate-limit each other on sign-in and signed API flows, which is a poor fit for the offline gym/studio scenario and the repo’s own documented concern.
+- Minimum actionable fix: Key throttling by a composite such as `{IP, stationToken}` for pre-auth paths and `{user/session, stationToken}` for authenticated paths, while retaining IP-level abuse caps.
+
+## 6. Security Review Summary
+
+- Authentication entry points: Partial Pass
+  - Evidence: `C:\Hale\Eaglepoint\Task-75\repo\backend\src\routes\auth.ts:46`, `C:\Hale\Eaglepoint\Task-75\repo\backend\src\services\auth-service.ts:252`, `C:\Hale\Eaglepoint\Task-75\repo\backend\src\services\auth-service.ts:328`, `C:\Hale\Eaglepoint\Task-75\repo\backend\src\services\auth-service.ts:379`
+  - Reasoning: Password login, bootstrap, PIN setup/re-entry, workstation binding, lockout, and warm lock are implemented. The main weakness found is per-IP throttling in a multi-station environment.
+
+- Route-level authorization: Pass
+  - Evidence: `C:\Hale\Eaglepoint\Task-75\repo\backend\src\app.ts:76`, `C:\Hale\Eaglepoint\Task-75\repo\backend\src\app.ts:81`, `C:\Hale\Eaglepoint\Task-75\repo\backend\src\app.ts:94`, `C:\Hale\Eaglepoint\Task-75\repo\backend\src\middleware.ts:192`
+  - Reasoning: Authenticated route groups are consistently wrapped in signed-session middleware and role checks where expected.
+
+- Object-level authorization: Partial Pass
+  - Evidence: `C:\Hale\Eaglepoint\Task-75\repo\backend\src\services\member-service.ts:149`, `C:\Hale\Eaglepoint\Task-75\repo\backend\src\services\face-service.ts:28`, `C:\Hale\Eaglepoint\Task-75\repo\backend\src\services\report-service.ts:569`
+  - Reasoning: Member, face, and inbox access are scoped by actor/member ownership or assignment. The coach/admin overview bug shows one self-route is misapplied across roles even though deeper checks stop data leakage.
+
+- Function-level authorization: Pass
+  - Evidence: `C:\Hale\Eaglepoint\Task-75\repo\backend\src\services\member-service.ts:85`, `C:\Hale\Eaglepoint\Task-75\repo\backend\src\services\content-service.ts:50`, `C:\Hale\Eaglepoint\Task-75\repo\backend\src\services\report-service.ts:55`
+  - Reasoning: Services add role/location/owner checks beyond route registration.
+
+- Tenant / user isolation: Partial Pass
+  - Evidence: `C:\Hale\Eaglepoint\Task-75\repo\backend\src\services\content-service.ts:26`, `C:\Hale\Eaglepoint\Task-75\repo\backend\src\services\member-service.ts:70`, `C:\Hale\Eaglepoint\Task-75\repo\backend\src\services\face-service.ts:68`
+  - Reasoning: User/location isolation exists; multi-tenant isolation is not a first-class concern in this single-facility/offline design. Shared-IP throttling still weakens workstation isolation operationally.
+
+- Admin / internal / debug protection: Pass
+  - Evidence: `C:\Hale\Eaglepoint\Task-75\repo\backend\src\app.ts:100`, `C:\Hale\Eaglepoint\Task-75\repo\backend\src\app.ts:105`, `C:\Hale\Eaglepoint\Task-75\repo\backend\src\routes\admin.ts:13`
+  - Reasoning: Admin routes are behind signed-session + admin role checks; no unprotected debug endpoints were found in the reviewed scope.
+
+## 7. Tests and Logging Review
+
+- Unit tests: Pass
+  - Evidence: `C:\Hale\Eaglepoint\Task-75\repo\backend\tests\security.test.ts:15`, `C:\Hale\Eaglepoint\Task-75\repo\backend\tests\member-service.test.ts:40`, `C:\Hale\Eaglepoint\Task-75\repo\backend\tests\face-service.test.ts:83`, `C:\Hale\Eaglepoint\Task-75\repo\backend\tests\report-service.test.ts:73`
+  - Reasoning: There is meaningful service/helper coverage for core business modules and failure paths.
+
+- API / integration tests: Partial Pass
+  - Evidence: `C:\Hale\Eaglepoint\Task-75\repo\backend\tests\routes.test.ts:292`, `C:\Hale\Eaglepoint\Task-75\repo\tests\e2e\app.spec.ts:72`, `C:\Hale\Eaglepoint\Task-75\repo\tests\e2e\bootstrap.spec.ts:7`
+  - Reasoning: There is good route/e2e breadth, but key route tests use service stubs, which allowed the admin/coach overview integration defect to slip through.
+
+- Logging categories / observability: Pass
+  - Evidence: `C:\Hale\Eaglepoint\Task-75\repo\backend\src\services\logging-service.ts:64`, `C:\Hale\Eaglepoint\Task-75\repo\backend\src\services\ops-service.ts:267`, `C:\Hale\Eaglepoint\Task-75\repo\backend\src\logger.ts:3`
+  - Reasoning: The backend has structured application/access/alert logging and an admin console aggregation surface.
+
+- Sensitive-data leakage risk in logs / responses: Pass
+  - Evidence: `C:\Hale\Eaglepoint\Task-75\repo\backend\src\services\logging-service.ts:3`, `C:\Hale\Eaglepoint\Task-75\repo\backend\src\logger.ts:6`, `C:\Hale\Eaglepoint\Task-75\repo\backend\tests\logging-service.test.ts:64`
+  - Reasoning: Sensitive fields are explicitly redacted in both persisted application logs and pino logger configuration.
+
+## 8. Test Coverage Assessment (Static Audit)
+
+### 8.1 Test Overview
+- Unit tests exist: backend Vitest and frontend Vitest.
+- API / integration tests exist: backend `supertest` route tests plus Playwright e2e specs.
+- Test frameworks: Vitest, Supertest, Playwright.
+- Test entry points: `repo/backend/package.json:9`, `repo/frontend/package.json:10`, `repo/package.json:6`.
+- Documentation provides test commands, but the canonical script is Docker-based and was not executed in this audit.
+- Evidence: `C:\Hale\Eaglepoint\Task-75\repo\backend\vitest.config.ts:1`, `C:\Hale\Eaglepoint\Task-75\repo\frontend\vitest.config.ts:1`, `C:\Hale\Eaglepoint\Task-75\repo\playwright.config.ts:1`, `C:\Hale\Eaglepoint\Task-75\repo\README.md:166`
+
+### 8.2 Coverage Mapping Table
+
+| Requirement / Risk Point | Mapped Test Case(s) | Key Assertion / Fixture / Mock | Coverage Assessment | Gap | Minimum Test Addition |
+| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
+| Password login, bootstrap, PIN re-entry, warm lock | `C:\Hale\Eaglepoint\Task-75\repo\backend\tests\routes.test.ts:293`, `C:\Hale\Eaglepoint\Task-75\repo\tests\e2e\app.spec.ts:225`, `C:\Hale\Eaglepoint\Task-75\repo\tests\e2e\bootstrap.spec.ts:7` | Session cookies, missing warm-session rejection, PIN setup/re-entry flow | basically covered | No test catches the admin/coach overview fetching member-only self profile after login | Add a frontend or integration test that logs in as coach/admin and asserts no `/api/self/profile` call or 404 occurs on overview |
+| Signed-session auth, nonce, timestamp, allowlist | `C:\Hale\Eaglepoint\Task-75\repo\backend\tests\routes.test.ts:350`, `C:\Hale\Eaglepoint\Task-75\repo\backend\tests\routes.test.ts:580`, `C:\Hale\Eaglepoint\Task-75\repo\backend\tests\routes.test.ts:737`, `C:\Hale\Eaglepoint\Task-75\repo\backend\tests\security.test.ts:15` | Signature missing/invalid, stale timestamp, replayed nonce, CIDR allowlist | sufficient | Multi-station shared-IP throttling behavior is not tested as a desired contract | Add tests for station-aware throttling once implemented |
+| RBAC and role-filtered navigation | `C:\Hale\Eaglepoint\Task-75\repo\backend\tests\routes.test.ts:608`, `C:\Hale\Eaglepoint\Task-75\repo\frontend\tests\App.spec.ts:80`, `C:\Hale\Eaglepoint\Task-75\repo\tests\e2e\app.spec.ts:121`, `C:\Hale\Eaglepoint\Task-75\repo\tests\e2e\app.spec.ts:211` | Admin-only route 403, reports/admin UI hidden from coach/member | basically covered | Route tests stub the member service and miss the expanded-role self-profile bug | Add an integration test with the real member service or a contract test around `/api/self/profile` for coach/admin users |
+| Member enrollment, coach assignment, face consent, phone masking | `C:\Hale\Eaglepoint\Task-75\repo\backend\tests\member-service.test.ts:64`, `C:\Hale\Eaglepoint\Task-75\repo\backend\tests\member-service.test.ts:184`, `C:\Hale\Eaglepoint\Task-75\repo\tests\e2e\app.spec.ts:72`, `C:\Hale\Eaglepoint\Task-75\repo\tests\e2e\app.spec.ts:155` | Encrypted phone flow, duplicate username rejection, assignment/consent updates, UI creation flow | basically covered | No direct test asserts phone remains contact-only with no verification/SMS workflow | Add a frontend/API test asserting no verification fields/endpoints are introduced in the enrollment flow |
+| Face consent, dedup, liveness, history, audit | `C:\Hale\Eaglepoint\Task-75\repo\backend\tests\face-service.test.ts:124`, `C:\Hale\Eaglepoint\Task-75\repo\backend\tests\face-service.test.ts:247`, `C:\Hale\Eaglepoint\Task-75\repo\backend\tests\face-service.test.ts:317`, `C:\Hale\Eaglepoint\Task-75\repo\backend\tests\routes.test.ts:654` | Consent required, duplicate preview redaction, quality/liveness failures, member-vs-other access | sufficient | Real camera/browser capture remains manual | Manual browser/hardware verification of camera capture/import |
+| Content analytics with date/location filtering and inline drill-down support | `C:\Hale\Eaglepoint\Task-75\repo\backend\tests\routes.test.ts:675`, `C:\Hale\Eaglepoint\Task-75\repo\backend\tests\routes.test.ts:699`, `C:\Hale\Eaglepoint\Task-75\repo\frontend\src\composables\useContentAnalytics.ts:51` | Invalid date rejection, out-of-scope location rejection, frontend MM/DD/YYYY parsing path | basically covered | No direct UI test for staying on-page while drill-down data updates | Add a component/composable test asserting chart selection updates drill-down rows without route change |
+| Dashboard/reporting/export/inbox/shared-folder alerts | `C:\Hale\Eaglepoint\Task-75\repo\backend\tests\report-service.test.ts:115`, `C:\Hale\Eaglepoint\Task-75\repo\backend\tests\report-service.test.ts:274`, `C:\Hale\Eaglepoint\Task-75\repo\tests\e2e\app.spec.ts:94`, `C:\Hale\Eaglepoint\Task-75\repo\tests\e2e\app.spec.ts:179` | Shared-folder failure alert, schedule loading, inbox access for non-admin subscriber, generate-now path | basically covered | Schedule creation path does not have a direct test for invalid subscriber/template IDs returning user-friendly errors | Add API tests for foreign-key/validation failures on schedule creation |
+| Logging redaction | `C:\Hale\Eaglepoint\Task-75\repo\backend\tests\logging-service.test.ts:64` | Asserts passwords, PINs, session tokens, biometric payloads, phone, IDs are redacted | sufficient | None material in reviewed scope | Keep regression tests as-is |
+
+### 8.3 Security Coverage Audit
+- Authentication: basically covered by route tests and warm-lock e2e, but not enough to catch the non-member overview misuse.
+- Route authorization: meaningfully covered for admin-only and inbox/non-admin cases; severe route-family regressions would likely be caught.
+- Object-level authorization: partially covered for members/faces/inbox; severe defects could still remain in less-tested cross-role flows like role-expanded self endpoints.
+- Tenant / data isolation: location/member scope is exercised, but there is no dedicated coverage for the shared-IP multi-station throttling risk.
+- Admin / internal protection: meaningfully covered by route tests; admin console/report authoring 403 boundaries are tested.
+
+### 8.4 Final Coverage Judgment
+- Partial Pass
+- Major risks covered: signed-session auth, nonce/timestamp/IP controls, core RBAC boundaries, member/face/report happy paths, many failure paths, and log redaction.
+- Major risks still uncovered enough that tests could pass while severe defects remain: cross-role self-profile behavior on default overview, station-aware throttling expectations for multi-terminal offline deployments, and some DB-backed validation/error handling around report schedule creation.
+
+## 9. Final Notes
+- The repository shows substantial real implementation effort and broad prompt coverage.
+- The strongest material defects found were documentation drift affecting static verifiability, the hidden default-overview failure for coach/admin users, and the IP-only throttling model for a multi-station offline environment.
+- Report written statically only; no runtime claims are made beyond what the code and tests prove.
*** End Patch
"}

   1: # SentinelFit Operations Platform
   2: 
   3: SentinelFit is a fully on-prem operations platform for gyms and training studios. This implementation delivers the planned vertical slices across authentication, member enrollment, biometric governance, coaching content, analytics, dashboarding, scheduled reporting, observability, and backup/recovery using a Docker-first runtime.
   4: 
   5: The implementation benchmark used for this repository is captured by the in-repo source, tests, and reviewer notes under [`backend`](./backend), [`frontend`](./frontend), and [`tests`](./tests).
   6: 
   7: ## Stack
   8: 
   9: - Frontend: Vue 3, TypeScript, Pinia, Tailwind CSS, TensorFlow.js, Chart.js
  10: - Backend: Express, TypeScript, Node.js, node-cron
  11: - Database: MySQL 8
  12: - Runtime: Docker Compose, fully offline/on-prem, no external API dependencies
  13: 
  14: ## Implemented modules
  15: 
  16: - Docker-first runtime with frontend, backend, primary MySQL, and standby MySQL
  17: - Password login, server-bound same-workstation PIN re-entry, warm-lock after inactivity, session expiry, and account-wide lockout handling
  18: - Signed request integrity with HMAC, nonce, and timestamp validation
  19: - Inclusive RBAC for Member, Coach, and Administrator
  20: - Member enrollment, coach assignment, masked phone handling, administrator/coach consent updates, member self-service face consent, and operator-entered member passwords with no shared UI default
  21: - Face enrollment with camera capture or image import, encrypted biometric artifacts, client-side quality guidance, pre-submit duplicate warnings, server-issued timed liveness challenges, server-derived face/liveness validation, dedup, versioning, deactivation, and immutable biometric audit records
  22: - Coach content publishing and analytics by station/location/date with MM/DD/YYYY filters, inline chart drill-down, and real view/search event capture
  23: - Admin dashboard layout persistence, configurable widget palette, and reusable report templates
  24: - Scheduled/offline reporting with subscriptions, inbox delivery, actionable downloads, and shared-folder output
  25: - Admin console for logs, alerts, latency/error metrics, backup, and dry-run restore
  26: - Encrypted local key vault with key version metadata and rotation support
  27: - Unit, integration-style, and Playwright end-to-end verification
  28: 
  29: ## Repository layout
  30: 
  31: - [frontend](./frontend): Vue application
  32: - [backend](./backend): Express API and background services
  33: - [tests/e2e](./tests/e2e): Playwright end-to-end tests
  34: - [docs](./docs): reviewer-facing API and design notes
  35: 
  36: ## Prerequisites
  37: 
  38: - Docker Desktop with Compose
  39: - Node.js 20+
  40: - npm 10+
  41: - Bash-compatible shell for `run_tests.sh`
  42: 
  43: ## Configuration
  44: 
  45: 1. Run with defaults directly via `docker compose up --build` for local/offline bootstrap on a fresh machine.
  46: 2. Optionally provide a `.env` file to override defaults for ports, credentials, shared-folder paths, or data directories.
  47: 
  48: Important environment variables:
  49: 
  50: - `MYSQL_DATABASE`
  51: - `MYSQL_USER`
  52: - `MYSQL_PASSWORD`
  53: - `MYSQL_ROOT_PASSWORD`
  54: - `MYSQL_PORT`
  55: - `MYSQL_PORT_BIND`
  56: - `MYSQL_HOST`
  57: - `MYSQL_STANDBY_HOST`
  58: - `MYSQL_STANDBY_PORT`
  59: - `MYSQL_STANDBY_PORT_BIND`
  60: - `BACKEND_PORT`
  61: - `BACKEND_NODE_ENV`
  62: - `BACKEND_ALLOWED_ORIGINS`
  63: - `BACKEND_DATA_DIR`
  64: - `BACKEND_REPORTS_SHARED_PATH`
  65: - `REPORTS_SHARED_HOST_PATH`
  66: - `BACKEND_IP_ALLOWLIST`
  67: - `BACKEND_API_RATE_LIMIT_PER_MINUTE`
  68: - `BACKEND_DEMO_SEED_USERS`
  69: - `BACKEND_KEY_VAULT_MASTER_KEY`
  70: - `FRONTEND_PORT`
  71: - `VITE_API_BASE_URL`
  72: 
  73: The default configuration allows both `localhost` and `127.0.0.1` frontend origins so browser verification remains stable in local and Docker-driven access paths.
  74: The shipped allowlist defaults cover local and private-LAN ranges so the offline deployment path does not start in an allow-all state.
  75: The Docker stack now binds report delivery to `REPORTS_SHARED_HOST_PATH` on the host so exported files are directly visible outside containers.
  76: 
  77: ## Run with Docker
  78: 
  79: The canonical startup command is:
  80: 
  81: ```bash
  82: docker compose up --build
  83: ```
  84: 
  85: Services:
  86: 
  87: - Frontend: [http://localhost:5173](http://localhost:5173)
  88: - Backend live health: [http://localhost:3000/health/live](http://localhost:3000/health/live)
  89: - Backend readiness: [http://localhost:3000/health/ready](http://localhost:3000/health/ready)
  90: - Primary MySQL: `localhost:3306`
  91: - Standby MySQL: `localhost:3307`
  92: 
  93: Shared report delivery:
  94: 
  95: - Host path: `${REPORTS_SHARED_HOST_PATH}` (default `./shared-reports`)
  96: - Container path: `${BACKEND_REPORTS_SHARED_PATH}` (default `/shared/reports`)
  97: - For Windows UNC/SMB shares, set `REPORTS_SHARED_HOST_PATH` to an absolute host-mounted path available to Docker Desktop.
  98: 
  99: ## Quick start (new machine)
 100: 
 101: 1. Install Docker Desktop (or Docker Engine + Compose plugin) and start Docker.
 102: 2. Open this repository directory.
 103: 3. Run:
 104: 
 105: ```bash
 106: docker compose up --build
 107: ```
 108: 
 109: 4. Wait until services are healthy, then open [http://localhost:5173](http://localhost:5173).
 110: 5. If `BACKEND_DEMO_SEED_USERS=false` (default), complete the one-time "First administrator setup".
 111: 6. To stop:
 112: 
 113: ```bash
 114: docker compose down
 115: ```
 116: 
 117: Notes:
 118: - No `.env` file is required for local startup; Compose defaults are provided.
 119: - Ensure ports `5173`, `3000`, `3306`, and `3307` are free on the host.
 120: 
 121: ## Troubleshooting
 122: 
 123: - Docker daemon not running:
 124:   Start Docker Desktop and retry `docker compose up --build`.
 125: 
 126: - Port already in use:
 127:   Find and stop conflicting processes, or override bind ports in `.env` (for example `FRONTEND_PORT`, `BACKEND_PORT`, `MYSQL_PORT_BIND`, `MYSQL_STANDBY_PORT_BIND`).
 128: 
 129: - Backend cannot connect to MySQL after config changes:
 130:   Recreate clean volumes:
 131:   ```bash
 132:   docker compose down --volumes --remove-orphans
 133:   docker compose up --build
 134:   ```
 135: 
 136: - Health endpoint not ready:
 137:   Check logs:
 138:   ```bash
 139:   docker compose logs backend
 140:   docker compose logs mysql
 141:   ```
 142:   Backend readiness endpoint: [http://localhost:3000/health/ready](http://localhost:3000/health/ready)
 143: 
 144: - UI loads but login/bootstrap fails:
 145:   Confirm backend is reachable at [http://localhost:3000/health/live](http://localhost:3000/health/live) and that station token input is set in the UI.
 146: 
 147: ## Demo seed accounts
 148: 
 149: Demo users are only created when `BACKEND_DEMO_SEED_USERS=true`.
 150: 
 151: - `admin` / `Admin12345!X`
 152: - `coach` / `Coach12345!X`
 153: - `member` / `Member12345!X`
 154: 
 155: The default non-demo runtime leaves seed users disabled so a clean deployment does not start with publicly documented credentials.
 156: 
 157: ## First-boot administrator setup
 158: 
 159: When `BACKEND_DEMO_SEED_USERS=false`, SentinelFit presents a one-time administrator bootstrap flow instead of relying on pre-seeded credentials.
 160: 
 161: 1. Start the stack with `docker compose up --build`
 162: 2. Open [http://localhost:5173](http://localhost:5173)
 163: 3. Complete the "First administrator setup" form
 164: 4. The created administrator is signed in immediately and the bootstrap path is closed once an administrator exists
 165: 
 166: ## Testing
 167: 
 168: The canonical unified test entrypoint is:
 169: 
 170: ```bash
 171: ./run_tests.sh
 172: ```
 173: 
 174: `run_tests.sh` performs:
 175: 
 176: - root dependency install
 177: - backend dependency install and coverage-checked tests
 178: - frontend dependency install and coverage-checked tests
 179: - Playwright browser install
 180: - `docker compose up --build -d`
 181: - readiness wait for frontend and backend
 182: - Playwright end-to-end tests
 183: - clean-install Docker restart with demo seeds disabled
 184: - first-boot Playwright bootstrap verification
 185: 
 186: Local verification commands are still useful, but Dockerized behavior is authoritative.
 187: Before running them directly, install package-local dependencies once:
 188: 
 189: ```bash
 190: cd backend && npm ci
 191: cd ../frontend && npm ci
 192: cd ..
 193: ```
 194: 
 195: Then run:
 196: 
 197: ```bash
 198: cd backend && npm test
 199: cd backend && npm run typecheck
 200: cd frontend && npm test
 201: cd frontend && npm run typecheck
 202: cd frontend && npm run build
 203: cd frontend && npm run preview
 204: npm run test:e2e
 205: ```
 206: 
 207: `npm run test:e2e` assumes frontend and backend services are already running and reachable on `127.0.0.1:5173` and `127.0.0.1:3000`. For a self-contained run, use `./run_tests.sh` (Docker canonical path).
 208: Local non-Docker checks validate source-level behavior and contracts, but deployment/runtime readiness is only guaranteed by the Docker-first path.
 209: 
 210: ### Frontend-only non-Docker review recipe
 211: 
 212: Use this path when Docker is unavailable and you only need frontend implementation validation:
 213: 
 214: ```bash
 215: cd frontend
 216: npm ci
 217: npm run typecheck
 218: npm test
 219: npm run build
 220: npm run dev
 221: ```
 222: 
 223: Coverage from this path:
 224: - Component/composable/store behavior and UI-level validation logic
 225: - Type safety and production frontend build output
 226: 
 227: Not covered by this path:
 228: - Backend/MySQL runtime integration
 229: - Playwright E2E against live backend services
 230: - Docker deployment readiness (`docker compose up --build`, `./run_tests.sh`)
 231: 
 232: ## Operational notes
 233: 
 234: - Sensitive fields are encrypted with AES-256 via a local key vault whose key material is envelope-encrypted at rest with `BACKEND_KEY_VAULT_MASTER_KEY`.
 235: - Key metadata is stored in MySQL and historical keys remain available for decrypt-on-read.
 236: - `KEY_VAULT_MASTER_KEY` defaults to `AUTO_GENERATE`, which creates a per-environment master key on first boot and stores it under the backend data directory.
 237: - `MYSQL_USER`, `MYSQL_PASSWORD`, and `MYSQL_ROOT_PASSWORD` have local bootstrap defaults for one-command startup; override them for production/shared deployments.
 238: - Persisted session secrets are encrypted and persisted session identifiers are stored as non-reusable hashes.
 239: - Biometric audit records are append-only and protected by MySQL triggers.
 240: - Stored biometric artifacts are encrypted before being written to disk.
 241: - Timed liveness challenges require the prompted head-turn capture to occur within the enforced server-side window.
 242: - Final face-in-frame and liveness acceptance are derived from image content on the server; client landmark clicks are guidance-only and never the source of truth.
 243: - The current biometric dedup/liveness layer uses lightweight local image heuristics suitable for offline baseline validation; high-assurance deployments should replace this with a stronger local face pipeline.
 244: - Face duplicate checks can be previewed before final enrollment submission so operators see warnings during capture review, not only after commit.
 245: - Preview-only dedup checks do not persist orphaned biometric artifacts to disk.
 246: - PIN re-entry is only valid when an active warm session already exists and the browser presents the server-issued workstation binding cookie; the station token remains attribution metadata, not the security proof.
 247: - PIN re-entry shares the sign-in rate limit path and lockout policy to reduce brute-force exposure.
 248: - Auth throttling keeps an IP-level abuse cap, but also keys per-station traffic so independent kiosks on the same LAN do not rate-limit each other by default.
 249: - Request-signing policy:
 250:   - Signed (`x-sf-timestamp` + `x-sf-nonce` + `x-sf-signature`) is required for all authenticated business routes under `/api/self`, `/api/members`, `/api/faces`, `/api/content`, `/api/dashboards`, `/api/reports`, and `/api/admin`.
 251:   - Unsigned exceptions are limited to pre-auth/session-establishment flows: `/api/auth/bootstrap/status`, `/api/auth/bootstrap/admin`, `/api/auth/login`, `/api/auth/pin/reenter`, and `/api/auth/session`.
 252:   - Compensating controls for unsigned exceptions include IP allowlist enforcement, station-aware auth throttling with an IP abuse cap, credential lockout policy, and workstation-binding cookie validation on `/api/auth/session`.
 253:   - Replay protection for signed routes uses nonce single-use records stored in MySQL with TTL, so replay history survives process restarts and multi-instance routing.
 254: - Session-state restoration uses the server session endpoint and keeps the signing secret in memory only, while warm-lock resumes require PIN re-entry.
 255: - Coach access scope is driven by explicit `coach_location_assignments` records rather than inferred from historical member/content data.
 256: - Analytics filters are validated server-side before query execution, including malformed date-range rejection.
 257: - Report exports are written to both a local backend reports directory and the configured shared-folder path.
 258: - Scheduled reports persist their configured export format (`CSV`/`Excel`/`PDF`) and cron runs execute with that same format.
 259: - Scheduled reports can target multiple subscribers, and inbox items expose direct download actions.
 260: - Subscriber inbox access is available to authenticated recipients while schedule/template authoring remains administrator-only.
 261: - Generated reports use the requesting administrator or schedule owner dashboard layout rather than a fixed administrator snapshot.
 262: - The Reports surface loads template state directly, so generation and scheduling do not depend on visiting Dashboards first.
 263: - Shared-folder delivery failures raise `storage_sync_error` alerts in the admin console.
 264: - Admin metrics surface uptime, request latency, server error rate, and recent backup/report timings.
 265: - Startup retries database initialization so `docker compose up` is resilient to MySQL readiness races.
 266: - Nightly encrypted backups and dry-run restore workflows target the standby MySQL instance.
 267: - The local key vault attempts to harden its file permissions on write; operators should still apply OS-level restrictions to the backend data directory.
 268: 
 269: ## Verification checklist
 270: 
 271: - `docker compose up --build` starts all services without manual intervention.
 272: - Backend readiness reports both API and database health.
 273: - Admin, coach, and member sign-in flows work with role-appropriate navigation.
 274: - `./run_tests.sh` passes end to end.
 275: - Playwright verifies core admin workflow plus coach/member access boundaries.

   1: # API Notes
   2: 
   3: This repository exposes a cookie-backed offline API rooted at `/api`.
   4: 
   5: ## Authentication
   6: 
   7: - `GET /api/auth/bootstrap/status`
   8:   Returns whether the first administrator still needs to be created.
   9: - `POST /api/auth/bootstrap/admin`
  10:   Creates the first administrator account and establishes a workstation-bound session.
  11: - `POST /api/auth/login`
  12:   Signs in with username and password.
  13: - `POST /api/auth/pin/reenter`
  14:   Resumes a warm-locked workstation session on the same bound station.
  15: - `GET /api/auth/session`
  16:   Restores the current session from cookies without signed headers. When a session is active it returns `session.currentUser`, `hasPin`, `warmLocked`, `sessionSecret` when unlocked, and timeout metadata.
  17: - `POST /api/auth/pin/setup`
  18:   Stores or rotates the current user's workstation PIN. Signed session required.
  19: - `POST /api/auth/warm-lock`
  20:   Warm-locks the current signed session. Signed session required.
  21: - `POST /api/auth/logout`
  22:   Revokes the current session and clears cookies. Signed session required.
  23: 
  24: ## Signed Routes
  25: 
  26: Signed request headers are required for:
  27: 
  28: - `/api/self/*`
  29: - `/api/members/*`
  30: - `/api/faces/*`
  31: - `/api/content/*`
  32: - `/api/dashboards/*`
  33: - `/api/reports/*`
  34: - `/api/admin/*`
  35: 
  36: All signed routes also require the workstation cookies from the active session.
  37: 
  38: ## Self-Service Member Routes
  39: 
  40: - `GET /api/self/profile`
  41:   Returns the caller's member profile when the authenticated user has a real `member_profiles` row.
  42: - `POST /api/self/consent/face`
  43:   Records the caller's face-enrollment consent for their member profile.

Output:
   1: # Design Notes
   2: 
   3: ## Session Model
   4: 
   5: - Sessions are established with `sf_session` and `sf_workstation` cookies.
   6: - `GET /api/auth/session` is the restore endpoint for existing browser sessions.
   7: - Signed business requests add HMAC headers on top of the workstation-bound cookies.
   8: - Warm-lock keeps the browser session but removes the in-memory signing secret until PIN re-entry succeeds.
   9: 
  10: ## Role Semantics
  11: 
  12: - Role inheritance is inclusive for authorization: administrators inherit coach and member capabilities, and coaches inherit member capabilities.
  13: - The frontend now distinguishes inherited roles from real member records with `currentUser.hasMemberProfile`.
  14: - Member-only self-service flows should rely on `hasMemberProfile`, not `roles.includes("Member")`, because staff accounts may not have a `member_profiles` row.
  15: 
  16: ## Rate Limiting
  17: 
  18: - Pre-auth traffic uses a station-aware composite key of `{ip, stationToken}` while still enforcing an IP-level cap.
  19: - Authenticated signed traffic uses a composite of `{ip, stationToken, user/session}` plus an IP-level cap.
  20: - This allows multiple gym terminals behind one LAN IP to operate independently without removing broad abuse protection.

330:  `CREATE TRIGGER biometric_audit_block_update BEFORE UPDATE ON biometric_audit_log

336:   SIGNAL SQLSTATE '45000' SET MESSAGE_TEXT = 'biometric_audit_log is immutable'`

339:export const migrationStatements: string[] = [
340:  `
341:SET @fk_bio_member_rule = (
342:  SELECT DELETE_RULE
343:  FROM INFORMATION_SCHEMA.REFERENTIAL_CONSTRAINTS
344:  WHERE CONSTRAINT_SCHEMA = DATABASE()
345:    AND TABLE_NAME = 'biometric_audit_log'
346:    AND CONSTRAINT_NAME = 'fk_bio_member'
347:  LIMIT 1
348:);
349:SET @needs_fk_bio_member_restrict = IF(@fk_bio_member_rule IS NULL OR @fk_bio_member_rule <> 'RESTRICT', 1, 0);
350:SET @drop_fk_bio_member = IF(
351:  @needs_fk_bio_member_restrict = 1,
352:  'ALTER TABLE biometric_audit_log DROP FOREIGN KEY fk_bio_member',
353:  'SELECT 1'
354:);
355:PREPARE stmt_drop_fk_bio_member FROM @drop_fk_bio_member;
356:EXECUTE stmt_drop_fk_bio_member;
357:DEALLOCATE PREPARE stmt_drop_fk_bio_member;
358:SET @add_fk_bio_member = IF(
359:  @needs_fk_bio_member_restrict = 1,
360:  'ALTER TABLE biometric_audit_log ADD CONSTRAINT fk_bio_member FOREIGN KEY (member_user_id) REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE RESTRICT',
361:  'SELECT 1'
362:);
363:PREPARE stmt_add_fk_bio_member FROM @add_fk_bio_member;
364:EXECUTE stmt_add_fk_bio_member;
365:DEALLOCATE PREPARE stmt_add_fk_bio_member;
366:`,
367:  `
368:SET @fk_bio_actor_rule = (
369:  SELECT DELETE_RULE

373:    AND CONSTRAINT_NAME = 'fk_bio_actor'
374:  LIMIT 1
375:);
376:SET @needs_fk_bio_actor_restrict = IF(@fk_bio_actor_rule IS NULL OR @fk_bio_actor_rule <> 'RESTRICT', 1, 0);
377:SET @drop_fk_bio_actor = IF(
378:  @needs_fk_bio_actor_restrict = 1,
379:  'ALTER TABLE biometric_audit_log DROP FOREIGN KEY fk_bio_actor',
380:  'SELECT 1'
381:);
382:PREPARE stmt_drop_fk_bio_actor FROM @drop_fk_bio_actor;
383:EXECUTE stmt_drop_fk_bio_actor;
384:DEALLOCATE PREPARE stmt_drop_fk_bio_actor;
385:SET @add_fk_bio_actor = IF(
386:  @needs_fk_bio_actor_restrict = 1,
387:  'ALTER TABLE biometric_audit_log ADD CONSTRAINT fk_bio_actor FOREIGN KEY (actor_user_id) REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE RESTRICT',
388:  'SELECT 1'
389:);
390:PREPARE stmt_add_fk_bio_actor FROM @add_fk_bio_actor;
391:EXECUTE stmt_add_fk_bio_actor;
392:DEALLOCATE PREPARE stmt_add_fk_bio_actor;

53:export const createDatabase = (config: AppConfig): Database => {
54:  const createPoolForCredentials = (user: string, password: string) => mysql.createPool({
55:    host: config.MYSQL_HOST,
56:    port: config.MYSQL_PORT,
57:    user,
58:    password,
59:    database: config.MYSQL_DATABASE,
60:    waitForConnections: true,
61:    connectionLimit: 10,
62:    maxIdle: 10,
63:    multipleStatements: true
64:  });
65:
66:  let pool = createPoolForCredentials(config.MYSQL_USER, config.MYSQL_PASSWORD);
67:
68:  return {
69:    get pool() {
70:      return pool;
71:    },
72:    async query<T extends RowDataPacket[]>(sql: string, params: unknown[] = []) {
73:      const [rows] = await pool.query<T>(sql, params);
74:      return rows;
75:    },
76:    async execute(sql: string, params: unknown[] = []) {
77:      await pool.execute(sql, params as never[]);
78:    },
79:    async executeInTransaction<T>(callback: (connection: PoolConnection) => Promise<T>) {
80:      const connection = await pool.getConnection();
81:      try {
82:        await connection.beginTransaction();
83:        const result = await callback(connection)…17 tokens truncated…t;
86:      } catch (error) {
87:        await connection.rollback();
88:        throw error;
89:      } finally {
90:        connection.release();
91:      }
92:    },
93:    async close() {
94:      await pool.end();
95:    },
96:    async ping() {
97:      try {
98:        await pool.query("SELECT 1");
99:        return true;
100:      } catch {
101:        return false;
102:      }
103:    },
104:    async initialize() {
105:      const initializeWithCurrentPool = async () => {
106:      await mkdir(config.DATA_DIR, { recursive: true });
107:      await mkdir(`${config.DATA_DIR}/uploads`, { recursive: true });
108:      await mkdir(`${config.DATA_DIR}/backups`, { recursive: true });
109:      await mkdir(config.REPORTS_SHARED_PATH, { recursive: true });
110:
111:      await pool.query(schemaStatements);
112:      for (const statement of migrationStatements) {
113:        await pool.query(statement);
114:      }
115:
116:      const ensureColumn = async (tableName: string, columnName: string, alterStatement: string) => {
117:        const [rows] = await pool.query<RowDataPacket[]>(
118:          `SELECT COLUMN_NAME
119:           FROM INFORMATION_SCHEMA.COLUMNS
120:           WHERE TABLE_SCHEMA = DATABASE()
121:             AND TABLE_NAME = ?
122:             AND COLUMN_NAME = ?
123:           LIMIT 1`,
124:          [tableName, columnName]
125:        );
126:
127:        if (rows.length === 0) {
128:          await pool.query(alterStatement);
129:        }
130:      };
131:
132:      await ensureColumn(
133:        "sessions",
134:        "station_token",
135:        `ALTER TABLE sessions
136:         ADD COLUMN station_token VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL DEFAULT 'Unknown-Station' AFTER session_secret`
137:      );
138:      await ensureColumn(
139:        "sessions",
140:        "session_secret_key_id",
141:        `ALTER TABLE sessions
142:         ADD COLUMN session_secret_key_id VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL DEFAULT 'legacy' AFTER session_secret`
143:      );
144:      await ensureColumn(
145:        "sessions",
146:        "workstation_binding_hash",
147:        `ALTER TABLE sessions
148:         ADD COLUMN workstation_binding_hash VARCHAR(255) NULL AFTER station_token`
149:      );
150:      await ensureColumn(
151:        "sessions",
152:        "warm_locked_at",
153:        `ALTER TABLE sessions
154:         ADD COLUMN warm_locked_at DATETIME NULL AFTER workstation_binding_hash`
155:      );
156:      await ensureColumn(
157:        "access_logs",
158:        "duration_ms",
159:        `ALTER TABLE access_logs
160:         ADD COLUMN duration_ms INT NOT NULL DEFAULT 0 AFTER status_code`
161:      );
162:      await ensureColumn(
163:        "report_schedules",
164:        "export_format",
165:        `ALTER TABLE report_schedules
166:         ADD COLUMN export_format VARCHAR(16) NOT NULL DEFAULT 'pdf' AFTER cron_expression`
167:      );
168:      await ensureColumn(
169:        "coach_location_assignments",
170:        "is_active",
171:        `ALTER TABLE coach_location_assignments
172:         ADD COLUMN is_active TINYINT(1) NOT NULL DEFAULT 1 AFTER location_code`
173:      );
174:      await ensureColumn(
175:        "face_record_versions",
176:        "center_image_key_id",
177:        `ALTER TABLE face_record_versions
178:         ADD COLUMN center_image_key_id VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL DEFAULT 'legacy' AFTER center_image_path`
179:      );
180:      await ensureColumn(
181:        "face_record_versions",
182:        "turn_image_key_id",
183:        `ALTER TABLE face_record_versions
184:         ADD COLUMN turn_image_key_id VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL DEFAULT 'legacy' AFTER turn_image_path`
185:      );
186:      await ensureColumn(
187:        "face_record_versions",
188:        "average_hash_key_id",
189:        `ALTER TABLE face_record_versions
190:         ADD COLUMN average_hash_key_id VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL DEFAULT 'legacy' AFTER average_hash`
191:      );
192:
193:      const [averageHashRows] = await pool.query<RowDataPacket[]>(
194:        `SELECT DATA_TYPE
195:         FROM INFORMATION_SCHEMA.COLUMNS
196:         WHERE TABLE_SCHEMA = DATABASE()
197:           AND TABLE_NAME = 'face_record_versions'
198:           AND COLUMN_NAME = 'average_hash'
199:         LIMIT 1`
200:      );
201:      if (averageHashRows[0] && String(averageHashRows[0].DATA_TYPE).toLowerCase() !== "text") {
202:        await pool.query(`ALTER TABLE face_record_versions MODIFY COLUMN average_hash TEXT NOT NULL`);
203:      }
204:
205:      for (const statement of triggerStatements) {
206:        await pool.query(statement);
207:      }
208:
209:      await pool.execute(
210:        `INSERT IGNORE INTO maintenance_mode (id, is_enabled, reason)
211:         VALUES (1, 0, 'normal_operation')`
212:      );
213:
214:      if (config.DEMO_SEED_USERS) {
215:        const seededUserIds = new Map<string, number>();
216:
217:        for (const user of seededUsers) {
218:          const passwordHash = await bcrypt.hash(user.password, 10);
219:          await pool.execute(
220:            `INSERT INTO users (username, full_name, password_hash)
221:             VALUES (?, ?, ?)
222:             ON DUPLICATE KEY UPDATE full_name = VALUES(full_name)`,
223:            [user.username, user.fullName, passwordHash]
224:          );
225:
226:          const [userRows] = await pool.query<RowDataPacket[]>(
227:            "SELECT id FROM users WHERE username = ? LIMIT 1",
228:            [user.username]
229:          );
230:          const userId = Number(userRows[0].id);
231:          seededUserIds.set(user.username, userId);
232:
233:          for (const role of user.roles) {
234:            await pool.execute(
235:              `INSERT IGNORE INTO user_roles (user_id, role_name) VALUES (?, ?)`,
236:              [userId, role]
237:            );
238:          }
239:
240:          if (user.roles.includes("Coach") || user.roles.includes("Administrator")) {
241:            await pool.execute(
242:              `INSERT IGNORE INTO coach_location_assignments (coach_user_id, location_code, is_active)
243:               VALUES (?, 'HQ', 1)`,
244:              [userId]
245:            );
246:          }
247:
248:          if (user.roles.includes("Member")) {
249:            await pool.execute(
250:              `INSERT IGNORE INTO member_profiles (user_id, location_code, notes) VALUES (?, ?, ?)`,
251:              [userId, user.locationCode ?? "HQ", user.notes ?? "Seeded member profile"]
252:            );
253:          }
254:        }
255:
256:        for (const user of seededUsers) {
257:          const memberUserId = seededUserIds.get(user.username);
258:          const coachUserId = user.coachUsername ? seededUserIds.get(user.coachUsername) : undefined;
259:
260:          if (memberUserId && coachUserId) {
261:            await pool.execute(
262:              `INSERT INTO coach_assignments (member_user_id, coach_user_id, is_active)
263:               SELECT ?, ?, 1
264:               FROM DUAL
265:               WHERE NOT EXISTS (
266:                 SELECT 1
267:                 FROM coach_assignments
268:                 WHERE member_user_id = ? AND coach_user_id = ? AND is_active = 1
269:               )`,
270:              [memberUserId, coachUserId, memberUserId, coachUserId]
271:            );
272:          }
273:
274:          const adminUserId = seededUserIds.get("admin");
275:          if (memberUserId && adminUserId && user.consentStatus) {
276:            await pool.execute(
277:              `INSERT INTO consent_records (member_user_id, consent_type, consent_status, recorded_by_user_id)
278:               SELECT ?, 'face_enrollment', ?, ?
279:               FROM DUAL
280:               WHERE NOT EXISTS (
281:                 SELECT 1
282:                 FROM consent_records
283:                 WHERE member_user_id = ?
284:                   AND consent_type = 'face_enrollment'
285:                   AND consent_status = ?
286:               )`,
287:              [memberUserId, user.consentStatus, adminUserId, memberUserId, user.consentStatus]
288:            );
289:          }
290:        }
291:      }
292:      };
293:
294:      await initializeWithCurrentPool();
295:    }
296:  };

### repo/backend/src/security.ts
1:import { createHash, createHmac, randomBytes, timingSafeEqual } from "node:crypto";
2:import { isIP } from "node:net";
3:import { AppError } from "./errors.js";
4:import type { AppConfig } from "./config.js";
5:
6:interface RateBucket {
7:  count: number;
8:  resetAt: number;
9:}
10:
11:export const createRateLimiter = () => {
12:  const buckets = new Map<string, RateBucket>();
13:
14:  return {
15:    check(key: string, limit: number, windowMs: number) {
16:      const now = Date.now();
17:      const bucket = buckets.get(key);
18:
19:      if (!bucket || bucket.resetAt <= now) {
20:        buckets.set(key, {
21:          count: 1,
22:          resetAt: now + windowMs
23:        });
24:        return;
25:      }
26:
27:      if (bucket.count >= limit) {
28:        throw new AppError(429, "rate_limited", "Rate limit exceeded");
29:      }
30:
31:      bucket.count += 1;
32:      buckets.set(key, bucket);
33:    }
34:  };
35:};
36:
37:export const createNonceStore = () => {
38:  const values = new Map<string, Map<string, number>>();
39:
40:  return {
41:    assertFresh(sessionToken: string, nonce: string, windowMs: number) {
42:      const now = Date.now();
43:      const sessionValues = values.get(sessionToken) ?? new Map<string, number>();
44:
45:      for (const [existingNonce, expiresAt] of sessionValues.entries()) {
46:        if (expiresAt <= now) {
47:          sessionValues.delete(existingNonce);
48:        }
49:      }
50:
51:      if (sessionValues.has(nonce)) {
52:        throw new AppError(401, "nonce_replayed", "The request nonce has already been used");
53:      }
54:
55:      sessionValues.set(nonce, now + windowMs);
56:      values.set(sessionToken, sessionValues);
57:    }
58:  };
59:};
60:
61:export const createSignaturePayload = (
62:  method: string,
63:  path: string,
64:  timestamp: string,
65:  nonce: string,
66:  body: unknown
67:) => {
68:  const bodyHash = createHash("sha256")
69:    .update(body ? JSON.stringify(body) : "")
70:    .digest("hex");
71:
72:  return [method.toUpperCase(), path, timestamp, nonce, bodyHash].join("\n");
73:};
74:
75:export const signPayload = (secret: string, payload: string) =>
76:  createHmac("sha256", Buffer.from(secret, "base64")).update(payload).digest("hex");
77:
78:export const verifySignature = (secret: string, payload: string, signature: string) => {
79:  const expected = Buffer.from(signPayload(secret, payload), "hex");
80:  const actual = Buffer.from(signature, "hex");
81:
82:  return expected.length === actual.length && timingSafeEqual(expected, actual);
83:};
84:
85:export const generateSessionToken = () => randomBytes(32).toString("hex");
86:
87:export const generateSessionSecret = () => randomBytes(32).toString("base64");
88:
89:export const generateWorkstationBindingToken = () => randomBytes(32).toString("hex");
90:
91:export const assertPasswordComplexity = (password: string) => {
92:  const complexityRegex = /^(?=.*[a-z])(?=.*[A-Z])(?=.*\d)(?=.*[^A-Za-z\d]).{12,}$/;
93:
94:  if (!complexityRegex.test(password)) {
95:    throw new AppError(
96:      400,
97:      "password_policy",
98:      "Passwords must be at least 12 characters and include upper, lower, number, and symbol"
99:    );
100:  }
101:};
102:
103:export const assertPinComplexity = (pin: string) => {
104:  if (!/^\d{4,6}$/.test(pin)) {
105:    throw new AppError(400, "pin_policy", "PIN must be 4 to 6 digits");
106:  }
107:};
108:
109:const normalizeIp = (value: string) => value.replace(/^::ffff:/, "");
110:
111:const ipv4ToInt = (value: string) =>
112:  normalizeIp(value)
113:    .split(".")
114:    .map((segment) => Number(segment))
115:    .reduce((accumulator, segment) => (accumulator << 8) + segment, 0) >>> 0;
116:
117:const matchesCidr = (ip: string, rule: string) => {
118:  const [range, prefixValue] = rule.split("/");
119:  if (!range || !prefixValue || isIP(normalizeIp(ip)) !== 4 || isIP(normalizeIp(range)) !== 4) {
120:    return false;
121:  }
122:
123:  const prefix = Number(prefixValue);
124:  if (!Number.isInteger(prefix) || prefix < 0 || prefix > 32) {
125:    return false;
126:  }
127:
128:  const mask = prefix === 0 ? 0 : ((0xffffffff << (32 - prefix)) >>> 0);
129:  return (ipv4ToInt(ip) & mask) === (ipv4ToInt(range) & mask);
130:};
131:
132:export const isIpAllowed = (config: AppConfig, ip: string) => {
133:  const normalizedIp = normalizeIp(ip);
134:  return config.IP_ALLOWLIST.some((rule) => {
135:    const normalizedRule = normalizeIp(rule);
136:    return normalizedRule.includes("/")
137:      ? matchesCidr(normalizedIp, normalizedRule)
138:      : normalizedRule === normalizedIp;
139:  });
140:};

45:  BACKUP_RETENTION_DAYS: z.coerce.number().int().min(1).default(30),
46:  KEY_ROTATION_DAYS: z.coerce.number().int().min(1).default(180),
47:  KEY_VAULT_MASTER_KEY: z.string().min(1),
48:  DEMO_SEED_USERS: z
49:    .string()
50:    .optional()
51:    .transform((value) => value === "true")
52:});
53:
54:export type AppConfig = z.infer<typeof configSchema>;
55:
56:export const loadConfig = (env: NodeJS.ProcessEnv = process.env): AppConfig => {
57:  const config = configSchema.parse({
58:    NODE_ENV: env.NODE_ENV,
59:    PORT: env.PORT,
60:    ALLOWED_ORIGINS: env.ALLOWED_ORIGINS,
61:    MYSQL_HOST: env.MYSQL_HOST,
62:    MYSQL_PORT: env.MYSQL_PORT,
63:    MYSQL_DATABASE: env.MYSQL_DATABASE,
64:    MYSQL_USER: env.MYSQL_USER,
65:    MYSQL_PASSWORD: env.MYSQL_PASSWORD,
66:    MYSQL_STANDBY_HOST: env.MYSQL_STANDBY_HOST,
67:    MYSQL_STANDBY_PORT: env.MYSQL_STANDBY_PORT,
68:    DATA_DIR: env.DATA_DIR,
69:    REPORTS_SHARED_PATH: env.REPORTS_SHARED_PATH,
70:    IP_ALLOWLIST: env.IP_ALLOWLIST,
71:    SESSION_TIMEOUT_MINUTES: env.SESSION_TIMEOUT_MINUTES,
72:    WARM_LOCK_MINUTES: env.WARM_LOCK_MINUTES,
73:    LOGIN_RATE_LIMIT_PER_MINUTE: env.LOGIN_RATE_LIMIT_PER_MINUTE,
74:    API_RATE_LIMIT_PER_MINUTE: env.API_RATE_LIMIT_PER_MINUTE,
75:    AUTH_RATE_LIMIT_PER_MINUTE: env.AUTH_RATE_LIMIT_PER_MINUTE,
76:    ACCOUNT_LOCK_MINUTES: env.ACCOUNT_LOCK_MINUTES,
77:    ACCOUNT_LOCK_MAX_ATTEMPTS: env.ACCOUNT_LOCK_MAX_ATTEMPTS,
78:    HMAC_WINDOW_MINUTES: env.HMAC_WINDOW_MINUTES,
79:    DEDUP_THRESHOLD: env.DEDUP_THRESHOLD,
80:    BACKUP_CRON: env.BACKUP_CRON,
81:    ACCESS_LOG_RETENTION_DAYS: env.ACCESS_LOG_RETENTION_DAYS,
82:    BIOMETRIC_AUDIT_RETENTION_DAYS: env.BIOMETRIC_AUDIT_RETENTION_DAYS,
83:    BACKUP_RETENTION_DAYS: env.BACKUP_RETENTION_DAYS,
84:    KEY_ROTATION_DAYS: env.KEY_ROTATION_DAYS,
85:    KEY_VAULT_MASTER_KEY: env.KEY_VAULT_MASTER_KEY,
86:    DEMO_SEED_USERS: env.DEMO_SEED_USERS
87:  });
88:
89:  if (config.NODE_ENV === "production" && config.IP_ALLOWLIST.length === 0) {
90:    throw new Error("IP_ALLOWLIST must be configured in production");
91:  }
92:  if (config.KEY_VAULT_MASTER_KEY === DEFAULT_MASTER_KEY_PLACEHOLDER) {
93:    throw new Error("KEY_VAULT_MASTER_KEY must be set to a non-placeholder 32-byte base64 value");
94:  }
95:
96:  if (config.NODE_ENV !== "test") {
97:    if (config.MYSQL_USER === DEFAULT_DB_USER_PLACEHOLDER) {
98:      throw new Error("MYSQL_USER must be set to a non-placeholder value");
99:    }
100:    if (config.MYSQL_PASSWORD === DEFAULT_DB_PASSWORD_PLACEHOLDER) {
101:      throw new Error("MYSQL_PASSWORD must be set to a non-placeholder value");
102:    }
103:    if (WEAK_DB_PASSWORDS.has(config.MYSQL_PASSWORD.toLowerCase())) {
104:      throw new Error("MYSQL_PASSWORD is too weak for runtime use; provide a strong per-environment password");
105:    }
106:  }
107:
108:  if (config.KEY_VAULT_MASTER_KEY !== AUTO_GENERATE_MASTER_KEY) {
109:    const decodedMasterKey = Buffer.from(config.KEY_VAULT_MASTER_KEY, "base64");
110:    if (decodedMasterKey.length !== 32 || decodedMasterKey.toString("base64") !== config.KEY_VAULT_MASTER_KEY) {
111:      throw new Error("KEY_VAULT_MASTER_KEY must be valid base64 and decode to exactly 32 bytes");
112:    }
113:  }
114:
115:  return config;
116:};

The backend surface is mostly coherent, so I’m switching to root-cause hunting now: object-level access checks, report/file handling, face-governance controls, and whether the frontend actually exposes the prompt’s major flows instead of just having service stubs.

Output:
1:# SentinelFit Operations Platform
2:
3:SentinelFit is a fully on-prem operations platform for gyms and training studios. This implementation delivers the planned vertical slices across authentication, member enrollment, biometric governance, coaching content, analytics, dashboarding, scheduled reporting, observability, and backup/recovery using a Docker-first runtime.
4:
5:The implementation benchmark used for this repository is captured by the in-repo source, tests, and reviewer notes under [`backend`](./backend), [`frontend`](./frontend), and [`tests`](./tests).
6:
7:## Stack
8:
9:- Frontend: Vue 3, TypeScript, Pinia, Tailwind CSS, TensorFlow.js, Chart.js
10:- Backend: Express, TypeScript, Node.js, node-cron
11:- Database: MySQL 8
12:- Runtime: Docker Compose, fully offline/on-prem, no external API dependencies
13:
14:## Implemented modules
15:
16:- Docker-first runtime with frontend, backend, primary MySQL, and standby MySQL
17:- Password login, server-bound same-workstation PIN re-entry, warm-lock after inactivity, session expiry, and account-wide lockout handling
18:- Signed request integrity with HMAC, nonce, and timestamp validation
19:- Inclusive RBAC for Member, Coach, and Administrator
20:- Member enrollment, coach assignment, masked phone handling, administrator/coach consent updates, member self-service face consent, and operator-entered member passwords with no shared UI default
21:- Face enrollment with camera capture or image import, encrypted biometric artifacts, client-side quality guidance, pre-submit duplicate warnings, server-issued timed liveness challenges, server-derived face/liveness validation, dedup, versioning, deactivation, and immutable biometric audit records
22:- Coach content publishing and analytics by station/location/date with MM/DD/YYYY filters, inline chart drill-down, and real view/search event capture
23:- Admin dashboard layout persistence, configurable widget palette, and reusable report templates
24:- Scheduled/offline reporting with subscriptions, inbox delivery, actionable downloads, and shared-folder output
25:- Admin console for logs, alerts, latency/error metrics, backup, and dry-run restore
26:- Encrypted local key vault with key version metadata and rotation support
27:- Unit, integration-style, and Playwright end-to-end verification
28:
29:## Repository layout
30:
31:- [frontend](./frontend): Vue application
32:- [backend](./backend): Express API and background services
33:- [tests/e2e](./tests/e2e): Playwright end-to-end tests
34:- [docs](./docs): reviewer-facing API and design notes
35:
36:## Prerequisites
37:
38:- Docker Desktop with Compose
39:- Node.js 20+
40:- npm 10+
41:- Bash-compatible shell for `run_tests.sh`
42:
43:## Configuration
44:
45:1. Run with defaults directly via `docker compose up --build` for local/offline bootstrap on a fresh machine.
46:2. Optionally provide a `.env` file to override defaults for ports, credentials, shared-folder paths, or data directories.
47:
48:Important environment variables:
49:
50:- `MYSQL_DATABASE`
51:- `MYSQL_USER`
52:- `MYSQL_PASSWORD`
53:- `MYSQL_ROOT_PASSWORD`
54:- `MYSQL_PORT`
55:- `MYSQL_PORT_BIND`
56:- `MYSQL_HOST`
57:- `MYSQL_STANDBY_HOST`
58:- `MYSQL_STANDBY_PORT`
59:- `MYSQL_STANDBY_PORT_BIND`
60:- `BACKEND_PORT`
61:- `BACKEND_NODE_ENV`
62:- `BACKEND_ALLOWED_ORIGINS`
63:- `BACKEND_DATA_DIR`
64:- `BACKEND_REPORTS_SHARED_PATH`
65:- `REPORTS_SHARED_HOST_PATH`
66:- `BACKEND_IP_ALLOWLIST`
67:- `BACKEND_API_RATE_LIMIT_PER_MINUTE`
68:- `BACKEND_DEMO_SEED_USERS`
69:- `BACKEND_KEY_VAULT_MASTER_KEY`
70:- `FRONTEND_PORT`
71:- `VITE_API_BASE_URL`
72:
73:The default configuration allows both `localhost` and `127.0.0.1` frontend origins so browser verification remains stable in local and Docker-driven access paths.
74:The shipped allowlist defaults cover local and private-LAN ranges so the offline deployment path does not start in an allow-all state.
75:The Docker stack now binds report delivery to `REPORTS_SHARED_HOST_PATH` on the host so exported files are directly visible outside containers.
76:
77:## Run with Docker
78:
79:The canonical startup command is:
80:
81:```bash
82:docker compose up --build
83:```
84:
85:Services:
86:
87:- Frontend: [http://localhost:5173](http://localhost:5173)
88:- Backend live health: [http://localhost:3000/health/live](http://localhost:3000/health/live)
89:- Backend readiness: [http://localhost:3000/health/ready](http://localhost:3000/health/ready)
90:- Primary MySQL: `localhost:3306`
91:- Standby MySQL: `localhost:3307`
92:
93:Shared report delivery:
94:
95:- Host path: `${REPORTS_SHARED_HOST_PATH}` (default `./shared-reports`)
96:- Container path: `${BACKEND_REPORTS_SHARED_PATH}` (default `/shared/reports`)
97:- For Windows UNC/SMB shares, set `REPORTS_SHARED_HOST_PATH` to an absolute host-mounted path available to Docker Desktop.
98:
99:## Quick start (new machine)
100:
101:1. Install Docker Desktop (or Docker Engine + Compose plugin) and start Docker.
102:2. Open this repository directory.
103:3. Run:
104:
105:```bash
106:docker compose up --build
107:```
108:
109:4. Wait until services are healthy, then open [http://localhost:5173](http://localhost:5173).
110:5. If `BACKEND_DEMO_SEED_USERS=false` (default), complete the one-time "First administrator setup".
111:6. To stop:
112:
113:```bash
114:docker compose down
115:```
116:
117:Notes:
118:- No `.env` file is required for local startup; Compose defaults are provided.
119:- Ensure ports `5173`, `3000`, `3306`, and `3307` are free on the host.
120:
121:## Troubleshooting
122:
123:- Docker daemon not running:
124:  Start Docker Desktop and retry `docker compose up --build`.
125:
126:- Port already in use:
127:  Find and stop conflicting processes, or override bind ports in `.env` (for example `FRONTEND_PORT`, `BACKEND_PORT`, `MYSQL_PORT_BIND`, `MYSQL_STANDBY_PORT_BIND`).
128:
129:- Backend cannot connect to MySQL after config changes:
130:  Recreate clean volumes:
131:  ```bash
132:  docker compose down --volumes --remove-orphans
133:  docker compose up --build
134:  ```
135:
136:- Health endpoint not ready:
137:  Check logs:
138:  ```bash
139:  docker compose logs backend
140:  docker compose logs mysql
141:  ```
142:  Backend readiness endpoint: [http://localhost:3000/health/ready](http://localhost:3000/health/ready)
143:
144:- UI loads but login/bootstrap fails:
145:  Confirm backend is reachable at [http://localhost:3000/health/live](http://localhost:3000/health/live) and that station token input is set in the UI.
146:
147:## Demo seed accounts
148:
149:Demo users are only created when `BACKEND_DEMO_SEED_USERS=true`.
150:
151:- `admin` / `Admin12345!X`
152:- `coach` / `Coach12345!X`
153:- `member` / `Member12345!X`
154:
155:The default non-demo runtime leaves seed users disabled so a clean deployment does not start with publicly documented credentials.
156:
157:## First-boot administrator setup
158:
159:When `BACKEND_DEMO_SEED_USERS=false`, SentinelFit presents a one-time administrator bootstrap flow instead of relying on pre-seeded credentials.
160:
161:1. Start the stack with `docker compose up --build`
162:2. Open [http://localhost:5173](http://localhost:5173)
163:3. Complete the "First administrator setup" form
164:4. The created administrator is signed in immediately and the bootstrap path is closed once an administrator exists
165:
166:## Testing
167:
168:The canonical unified test entrypoint is:
169:
170:```bash
171:./run_tests.sh
172:```
173:
174:`run_tests.sh` performs:
175:
176:- root dependency install
177:- backend dependency install and coverage-checked tests
178:- frontend dependency install and coverage-checked tests
179:- Playwright browser install
180:- `docker compose up --build -d`
181:- readiness wait for frontend and backend
182:- Playwright end-to-end tests
183:- clean-install Docker restart with demo seeds disabled
184:- first-boot Playwright bootstrap verification
185:
186:Local verification commands are still useful, but Dockerized behavior is authoritative.
187:Before running them directly, install package-local dependencies once:
188:
189:```bash
190:cd backend && npm ci
191:cd ../frontend && npm ci
192:cd ..
193:```
194:
195:Then run:
196:
197:```bash
198:cd backend && npm test
199:cd backend && npm run typecheck
200:cd frontend && npm test
201:cd frontend && npm run typecheck
202:cd frontend && npm run build
203:cd frontend && npm run preview
204:npm run test:e2e
205:```
206:
207:`npm run test:e2e` assumes frontend and backend services are already running and reachable on `127.0.0.1:5173` and `127.0.0.1:3000`. For a self-contained run, use `./run_tests.sh` (Docker canonical path).
208:Local non-Docker checks validate source-level behavior and contracts, but deployment/runtime readiness is only guaranteed by the Docker-first path.
209:
210:### Frontend-only non-Docker review recipe
211:
212:Use this path when Docker is unavailable and you only need frontend implementation validation:
213:
214:```bash
215:cd frontend
216:npm ci
217:npm run typecheck
218:npm test
219:npm run build
220:npm run dev
221:```
222:
223:Coverage from this path:
224:- Component/composable/store behavior and UI-level validation logic
225:- Type safety and production frontend build output
226:
227:Not covered by this path:
228:- Backend/MySQL runtime integration
229:- Playwright E2E against live backend services
230:- Docker deployment readiness (`docker compose up --build`, `./run_tests.sh`)
231:
232:## Operational notes
233:
234:- Sensitive fields are encrypted with AES-256 via a local key vault whose key material is envelope-encrypted at rest with `BACKEND_KEY_VAULT_MASTER_KEY`.
235:- Key metadata is stored in MySQL and historical keys remain available for decrypt-on-read.
236:- `KEY_VAULT_MASTER_KEY` defaults to `AUTO_GENERATE`, which creates a per-environment master key on first boot and stores it under the backend data directory.
237:- `MYSQL_USER`, `MYSQL_PASSWORD`, and `MYSQL_ROOT_PASSWORD` have local bootstrap defaults for one-command startup; override them for production/shared deployments.
238:- Persisted session secrets are encrypted and persisted session identifiers are stored as non-reusable hashes.
239:- Biometric audit records are append-only and protected by MySQL triggers.
240:- Stored biometric artifacts are encrypted before being written to disk.
241:- Timed liveness challenges require the prompted head-turn capture to occur within the enforced server-side window.
242:- Final face-in-frame and liveness acceptance are derived from image content on the server; client landmark clicks are guidance-only and never the source of truth.
243:- The current biometric dedup/liveness layer uses lightweight local image heuristics suitable for offline baseline validation; high-assurance deployments should replace this with a stronger local face pipeline.
244:- Face duplicate checks can be previewed before final enrollment submission so operators see warnings during capture review, not only after commit.
245:- Preview-only dedup checks do not persist orphaned biometric artifacts to disk.
246:- PIN re-entry is only valid when an active warm session already exists and the browser presents the server-issued workstation binding cookie; the station token remains attribution metadata, not the security proof.
247:- PIN re-entry shares the sign-in rate limit path and lockout policy to reduce brute-force exposure.
248:- Auth throttling keeps an IP-level abuse cap, but also keys per-station traffic so independent kiosks on the same LAN do not rate-limit each other by default.
249:- Request-signing policy:
250:  - Signed (`x-sf-timestamp` + `x-sf-nonce` + `x-sf-signature`) is required for all authenticated business routes under `/api/self`, `/api/members`, `/api/faces`, `/api/content`, `/api/dashboards`, `/api/reports`, and `/api/admin`.
251:  - Unsigned exceptions are limited to pre-auth/session-establishment flows: `/api/auth/bootstrap/status`, `/api/auth/bootstrap/admin`, `/api/auth/login`, `/api/auth/pin/reenter`, and `/api/auth/session`.
252:  - Compensating controls for unsigned exceptions include IP allowlist enforcement, station-aware auth throttling with an IP abuse cap, credential lockout policy, and workstation-binding cookie validation on `/api/auth/session`.
253:  - Replay protection for signed routes uses nonce single-use records stored in MySQL with TTL, so replay history survives process restarts and multi-instance routing.
254:- Session-state restoration uses the server session endpoint and keeps the signing secret in memory only, while warm-lock resumes require PIN re-entry.
255:- Coach access scope is driven by explicit `coach_location_assignments` records rather than inferred from historical member/content data.
256:- Analytics filters are validated server-side before query execution, including malformed date-range rejection.
257:- Report exports are written to both a local backend reports directory and the configured shared-folder path.
258:- Scheduled reports persist their configured export format (`CSV`/`Excel`/`PDF`) and cron runs execute with that same format.
259:- Scheduled reports can target multiple subscribers, and inbox items expose direct download actions.
260:- Subscriber inbox access is available to authenticated recipients while schedule/template authoring remains administrator-only.
261:- Generated reports use the requesting administrator or schedule owner dashboard layout rather than a fixed administrator snapshot.
262:- The Reports surface loads template state directly, so generation and scheduling do not depend on visiting Dashboards first.
263:- Shared-folder delivery failures raise `storage_sync_error` alerts in the admin console.
264:- Admin metrics surface uptime, request latency, server error rate, and recent backup/report timings.
265:- Startup retries database initialization so `docker compose up` is resilient to MySQL readiness races.
266:- Nightly encrypted backups and dry-run restore workflows target the standby MySQL instance.
267:- The local key vault attempts to harden its file permissions on write; operators should still apply OS-level restrictions to the backend data directory.
268:
269:## Verification checklist
270:
271:- `docker compose up --build` starts all services without manual intervention.
272:- Backend readiness reports both API and database health.
273:- Admin, coach, and member sign-in flows work with role-appropriate navigation.
274:- `./run_tests.sh` passes end to end.
275:- Playwright verifies core admin workflow plus coach/member access boundaries.
